title: Network Specialist
description:
Focus of the Role

This role is critical in ensuring the cybersecurity integrity and operational continuity of Telstra’s global transmission services. Responsible for detecting, investigating, and responding to Submarine and transmission incidents impacting the network infrastructure including cybersecurity concerns. Our Technical team use the latest most cutting-edge technologies to maintain our network 24x7, 365 days a year ensuring that we effectively meet high availability of Networks and Services with particular focus on the Transmission Submarine and Terrestrial core networks and edges.

What You’ll Do

  • Provide NOC services to other Cables and OTT customers, generate monthly reports for Indigo Cable (Singapore-Perth), EAC Segment-M Qingdao, and other Reports as required
  • Provide technical support and service solutions for Telstra’s transmission networks, including submarine cable systems, DWDM/SLTE, Ethernet, and leased services.
  • Perform 24x7 network monitoring, fault investigation, service restoration, and escalation to meet SLA and KPI targets.
  • Monitor and respond to cybersecurity threats, coordinating incident response with Telstra’s Security Operations Centre (SOC).
  • Proactively monitor network performance, conduct preventive maintenance, and analyze system data to ensure compliance with performance standards.
  • Coordinate with vendors, cable consortiums, and field teams for fault resolution, equipment replacement, and repair activities.
  • Prepare reports, manage notifications for planned/unplanned events, and ensure compliance with global regulatory requirements.
  • Support business continuity exercises, emergency restorations, and network augmentation projects in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

About You

  • Submarine & Optical Network Expertise
    Strong understanding of submarine and terrestrial cable systems, DWDM, OTN, SDH, Ethernet, and Layer 0/1 technologies.
  • Fault Management & Troubleshooting
    Ability to diagnose network issues, interpret alarm hierarchies, and perform restoration activities under pressure.
  • Vendor & Platform Knowledge
    Experience with major optical network platforms (Infinera, Ciena, Nokia, Alcatel, Ericsson) and associated NMS tools.
  • Tools & Technical Proficiency
    Familiarity with service inventory systems, incident/change management tools, testing equipment, and MS Office suite; basic programming and data analytics skills (Python, Tableau, Splunk).
  • Communication & Collaboration
    Excellent interpersonal skills, ability to manage customer escalations, and coordinate with multiple stakeholders globally.
  • Adaptability & Customer Focus
    Ability to work in a 24x7 environment, handle shift changes, and maintain high standards of customer service and professionalism.
  • Education & Experience
    Bachelor’s degree in Electronics/Communications Engineering (or related field) with 5+ years in submarine/optical network operations.

postedCompany.description:
TELSTRA SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.

Telstra Global is a leading global supplier of managed network services and international data, voice and satellite services. It is a division of the leading Australia-based, tier 1 telecommunications and media services company, Telstra Corporation Limited, and owns one of the most technologically advanced IP backbone networks in the world.

Telstra provides global telecommunications services and solutions and is an expert in bringing the advantages of customer centric managed network solutions to the business community. Together with its offshore subsidiaries and international investments, Telstra serves many the world's top companies, spanning Europe, Asia Pacific and the Americas. Telstra Global is ideally suited to provide your IT solutions, with its experience and expertise; security and reliability; trust and financial strength.

Telstra operates award-winning networks which are amongst the largest and most diverse in Asia Pacific. We have operating licences and landing rights in most major Asian markets, the US and EMEA, facilitating access to over 1,400 PoPs in 230 countries and territories.
